name: Diamond Head Market & Grill
tagline: Monsarrat Ave institution since the '90s — the lemon crunch cake alone is worth the trip.
mustOrder: lemon crunch cake — legendary, the signature
parking: Small lot behind the market; street parking on Monsarrat Ave
talkStory: Opened in 1997 by Chef Kelvin Ro, Diamond Head Market & Grill became a Monsarrat Ave institution with its takeout window serving plate lunches, poke, and the now-iconic lemon crunch cake — locals order full cakes for parties and holidays. Tourists discovered it thanks to the Diamond Head hike route, but this is a legit neighborhood staple. The insider move: go weekday mornings to skip the post-hike weekend rush. The market side has great grab-and-go onigiri too.
